title: rubus neomalacus
intro: Rubus neomalacus, also known as the wild black raspberry or thimbleberry, is a type of shrub that belongs to the rose family. It is native to North America and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus neomalacus include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Rubus neomalacus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in jams and preserves due to its sweet and tart flavor.
